name: MetLife Stadium
tagline: This is where it all ends: the first-ever 48-team World Cup Final lands at the biggest stadium of the tournament.

why.title: Hosts the Final
why.text: MetLife stages the July 19 World Cup Final, the showpiece match of the entire tournament. The road to the trophy ends right here.

why.title: Biggest in the Cup
why.text: At ~80,800 seats it's the largest venue at WC2026, and the largest stadium in the NFL. Size is exactly why it landed the final.

why.title: Turned Turf to Grass
why.text: Crews buried the artificial turf under two feet of sand and stitched in a hybrid Bermudagrass pitch built to survive eight matches in summer heat.

why.title: Proven Big-Match Stage
why.text: It already hosted the 2016 Copa America Centenario final and the 2025 Club World Cup final. This building knows how to throw a championship.

gettingThere: Take the NJ Transit train, not a car. From Manhattan, ride from Penn Station to Secaucus Junction, then hop the Meadowlands shuttle that drops you at the stadium's front door in about 10 minutes.
transit: Meadowlands Rail Line runs straight to the stadium for every WC match; transfer at Secaucus Junction (marked 'SEC').
Penn Station NYC to Secaucus to stadium runs ~45-60 min, ~$12-15 each way.
Coach USA 351 'Meadowlands Express' bus runs from Port Authority to Lot K, starting ~2.5 hours before kickoff.
Driving is the backup, not the plan: event parking ~$40-60 and final-day lots already sold out.
matchday: There's no real neighborhood around the stadium, so the party lives across the river: FIFA's official NY/NJ Fan Festival, a converted mini-pitch at Rockefeller Center (Jul 6-19), and a free fan hub at Sports Illustrated Stadium in Harrison. Fans pre-game in NYC, Hoboken and Jersey City bars, then funnel onto the trains.
food: Jones Wood Foundry (Upper East Side): British gastropub and the prime England-supporters spot.
Smithfield Hall (Chelsea): a soccer-bar institution home to eight European supporters' clubs.
Football Factory at Legends (Koreatown): three levels of soccer-only chaos for big matches.
Hudson Hall (Jersey City): a big Eastern-European beerhall that takes reservations, ideal for groups on the NJ side.
tips: Beat the post-match train crush: either sprint to the platform the instant the whistle blows, or linger 30-45 min to let the first wave clear. The slow middle is the worst.
Load your return fare BEFORE the match so you're not stuck in a ticket line with 80,000 people.
Arrive 2-3 hours early for the final: airport-style security on 80k fans and a tighter perimeter make gates brutal.
Clear-bag policy is strict: clear plastic bag max 12x6x12, a gallon Ziploc, or a tiny clutch. No backpacks.
For the 3 PM July final, the west-facing upper deck bakes in full sun; aim for east-side or lower-bowl shade if heat-sensitive.
Base in Hoboken or Jersey City for cheaper rooms and a shorter transit hop than Manhattan.
weather: Expect a hot, humid mid-summer afternoon (June ~77F, July final ~87F) in full open-air sun, so pack sunscreen, a hat, and a sealed clear water bottle under 20 oz.
lore: Copa America Centenario Final 2016: Chile beat Argentina on penalties, the biggest soccer final here before the World Cup.
Hosted Super Bowl XLVIII (2014), the first cold-weather Super Bowl in the NY/NJ area.
Staged the 2025 FIFA Club World Cup Final, the venue's full dress rehearsal for the big one.
A top-tier concert and WrestleMania stage (WrestleMania 29 and 35), built for ~$1.6B as the priciest US stadium ever at completion.
